events.description: Senator Murray introduced S. 716 to authorize issuance of patent in fee to George Peters; bill read twice and referred to Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of patent in fee, in reply to Peters' letter of January 20th
Department of the Interior (Oscar L. Chapman, Undersecretary) issued report on S. 716, recommending enactment with amendments to allow sale to a Crow Indian rather than issuance of patent in fee
Joseph C. O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report and asking if amendments were agreeable
Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment and recommended the bill do pass; committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed as Report No. 508
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of the House Committee on Public Lands; enclosed copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Telegram sent to George Peters at Wyola, Montana, advising that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of the Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
George Peters indicated his desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian
A similar bill was introduced in the 80th Congress to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to issue a patent in fee to George Peters for 840 acres on the Crow Reservation

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of George Peters' 840-acre allotment on Crow Indian Reservation to another Crow Indian, authorized under S. 716 as amended
